asa-autobots:2-design:p2p-manager-code.text: note that the two agents should cooperate and act on the same knowledge, meaning that if one agent is going to pick up parcel 2 then the other ideally shouldn't consider going to pick up that as it's what the other is doing, so I want to have some shared knowledge that each can "claim" or something along those lines to avoid racing to achieve the same thing and therefore getting stuck

also with this in mind is it better to have separate scripts for the agents and a script for the state handler or evaluation engine which can be queried for the information or is it better to duplicate it and sync it? I don't know which would be best for access and ease of use

deadlock-avoidance.text: for path overlap you need to have a "future overlap prediction" because imagine we overlap, but I reach the overlap in 3 moves while you reach it in 30, yeah there may be an overlap but it is highly unlikely sice by the time you'd get there I'd have already gone away, also this shouldn't compromise other pathing

sandbox-ctrls.text: instead of changing between direct messaging and agentic loop from the top I want it near where the button to send is placed since the actual content doesn't change that much

p2p-manager-code.text: instead of parsing with regexes the p2p messaging should be handled in json and therefore can be parsed to correctly match and reason about the intentions

p2p-state-analysis.text: also note that for the llm side of reasoning we want to only accept llm answers that match our format, that being for now json as it's easily parsable and checkable, for this reason the prompt needs to explicitly instrucut on how the message should be created and what their structure should look like. For these reasons we from the parsing side we should discard invalid answers, create some knowledge on how the llm should fix them, for example giving it the parsing error and add it to the list of history and ask it for a new answer
